summary: DIAGNOSIS: Could NOT reproduce the reported broken-preview bug for Quest image upload. Uploaded both a WEBP and a PNG via the Create Quest 'Image (optional)' Choose file input as teacher test_teacher_1 in Demo Class. Preview rendered correctly, quest was created, and the child (classmate_g3) sees the image successfully on the Quest Board.
conclusion: The frontend flow works exactly as intended: uploadFile() posts to /api/upload/quest-asset, receives {url:'/api/uploads/quests/.'}, sets questForm.image_url = res.url, and getAssetUrl() correctly prepends REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL to produce 'https:///api/uploads/quests/.'. The loads with naturalWidth>0 (not broken). Backend GET returns 200 with correct image content-type. Child sees the image too.
hypotheses_for_teachers_original_report: Teacher may have been on an OLD build/cached JS before a recent fix (getAssetUrl was previously missing the /api/ prefix handling). Ask them to hard-refresh.

Teacher's original webp may have been >512KB, taking the chunked path — but chunked-complete also returns {url:'/api/uploads/quests/...'} so this should still work. Worth double-checking with a real >512KB webp if the report specifically mentions large files.
If teacher tried an image WHILE offline / on a network that blocks the emergent preview subdomain, the would show broken glyph while backend curl (from server-side) still succeeds.
Possible transient CORS/cookie issue if teacher was in an iframe context; browser withCredentials fetch to /api/uploads/... would be affected. However, my test succeeded with credentials:'include'.

issues: React warning: Missing Description/aria-describedby on DialogContent (accessibility only, non-blocking)

action_items: No code fix needed — bug NOT REPRODUCIBLE on current deployed build.

Ask reporter to hard-refresh their browser (Ctrl+Shift+R) and retry. If still broken, ask for: (a) exact upload response body from Network tab; (b) exact preview img src; (c) file size and mime type of the webp; (d) any console errors.
critical_code_review_comments: TeacherDashboard.jsx is 2331 lines — should be split into smaller focused modules.
handleQuestFileUpload silently swallows error details ('Upload failed') — showing error.response?.data?.detail would help diagnose future upload failures for users.
seed_data_creation: Created a quest 'TEST_Diag Quest' in Demo Class with a small PNG image and completed the child-side visibility check. Cleanup recommended if noisy.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Login flow for teacher via UI is flaky in headless (session cookie not set from /login form submit). Workaround: POST /api/auth/login with {identifier, password} directly via page.evaluate, then localStorage.setItem('session_token', ...). Then goto /teacher-dashboard. Same pattern for child logins. Test images at /tmp/tiny.png and /tmp/tiny.webp (84 bytes each).
rca_of_the_issue: NO BUG FOUND on current build. Upload endpoint returns {url:'/api/uploads/quests/<hash>.<ext>'}. Frontend stores this exactly as-is in questForm.image_url. getAssetUrl() sees path starts with '/api/' and returns `${BACKEND_URL}${path}` = 'https://coinquest-staging.preview.emergentagent.com/api/uploads/quests/<hash>.<ext>'. Browser GET returns HTTP 200 with correct image/webp or image/png content-type. Preview img.naturalWidth is 20 (not 0), img.complete is true. Child GET of same URL returns 200 and renders. The URL is NOT mangled at any step.
